mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity risk premiums compress and discount rates fall, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.22% as of 2009-02-15 indicates maximum easing
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration. Persistent payroll losses exceeding 500k persons in a single month trigger equity sell-offs as investors anticipate recession deepening and corporate earnings compression over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 500 thousand persons in one month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133768 thousand as of 2009-02-15 reflects ongoing job losses
regime_hint: post-shock recession with accelerating labor shedding
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances following months with PAYEMS declines > 500k, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 5% month-over-month, the interaction indicates broad-based demand destruction that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 346810M and HOUST at 466k as of 2009-02-15 show demand weakness
regime_hint: demand-side recession with synchronized consumer and housing collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 5%, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals supply-side stress and capacity utilization collapse. When INDPRO declines more than 2% month-over-month, equity investors interpret this as evidence of demand weakness and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.337 as of 2009-02-15 reflects industrial contraction
regime_hint: supply-side recession with collapsing manufacturing activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following INDPRO declines > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure labor market slack. When UNRATE rises above 8% AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month, the interaction signals accelerating joblessness that triggers equity sell-offs as consumer spending and corporate earnings face headwinds over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.1% and PAYEMS declining as of 2009-02-15
regime_hint: labor market crisis with simultaneous unemployment surge and job losses
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both UNRATE > 8% and PAYEMS declines, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) near or below prior-year levels signals deflationary pressure. When year-over-year CPI growth falls below 1%, the Fed maintains accommodative policy and equity valuations expand as real discount rates decline, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.174 as of 2009-01-15 reflects disinflationary environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when CPI year-over-year growth < 1%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) contraction signals recession. When quarterly real GDP growth turns negative or falls below -1% annualized rate, equity investors anticipate earnings recession and corporate margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11525.0B as of 2008-10-15 reflects recession onset
regime_hint: demand-side recession with negative GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances following quarters with GDPC1 growth < -1% annualized, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) always-on trend signal. Sustained month-over-month growth in both series indicates robust consumer demand and housing recovery. This persistent demand strength drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations and multiple expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show weakness as of 2009-02-15 but mechanism applies when both trend positive
regime_hint: demand-side recovery with synchronized housing and consumer strength
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month growth.
